title: Designing in the open
description: A few thoughts on sharing work early and often.
excerpt: Publishing rough drafts, gathering feedback, and letting the work evolve in public.
author: Pagewright

props.html:
There's a particular kind of momentum that comes from working in the open. When the cost of publishing drops to nearly zero, you start to share earlier — a half-formed idea, a rough sketch, a first draft.

Lower the cost of shipping

The friction of publishing shapes what you publish. If it takes an afternoon of fiddling to get a post live, you'll only post the big things. If it takes a click, you'll share the small ones too — and the small ones compound.

Make the easy thing the right thing.

That's the whole idea: a visual editor for the words, and automation for everything else.
